content:
# Luke Chapter 13 — Translation Disagreements

**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **Translators:** EMET (Literal), LOGOS (Formal), PNEUMA (Dynamic)

---

## Verse-by-Verse Disagreements

### 13:1 — "at that very time" vs. "at that very season"
| Translator | Rendering | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "at that very season" | Preserves *kairos* as "season" |
| LOGOS | "at that very time" | Standard temporal rendering |
| PNEUMA | "At that very time" | Agrees with LOGOS |
| **SOPHIA** | **"at that very time"** | **LOGOS/PNEUMA (2-to-1); "time" is natural for narrative temporal clause** |

### 13:1 — "mingled" vs. "mixed"
| Translator | Rendering | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "mixed" | Standard rendering of *emixen* |
| LOGOS | "mingled" | More formal register |
| PNEUMA | "mixed" | Agrees with EMET |
| **SOPHIA** | **"mingled"** | **LOGOS selected; higher register suits the gravity of sacrilege** |

### 13:4 — "debtors" vs. "guiltier"
| Translator | Rendering | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "debtors" | Literal *opheiletai* |
| LOGOS | "debtors" | Preserves debt-sin distinction |
| PNEUMA | "guiltier" | Dynamic equivalent |
| **SOPHIA** | **"debtors"** | **EMET/LOGOS (2-to-1); preserves shift from "sinners" in v. 2** |

### 13:5 — "in like manner" vs. "similarly" vs. "in the same way"
| Translator | Rendering | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "similarly" | Renders *hosautos* |
| LOGOS | "in like manner" | Distinguishes from *homoios* in v. 3 |
| PNEUMA | "in the same way" | Dynamic equivalent |
| **SOPHIA** | **"in like manner"** | **LOGOS selected; preserves variation between vv. 3 and 5** |

### 13:7 — "vinedresser" vs. "vine-dresser" vs. "vineyard keeper"
| Translator | Rendering | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "vine-dresser" | Hyphenated compound |
| LOGOS | "vinedresser" | Single word |
| PNEUMA | "vineyard keeper" | Two-word paraphrase |
| **SOPHIA** | **"vinedresser"** | **LOGOS selected; established single-word form** |

### 13:7 — "render the ground useless" vs. "render-useless the ground" vs. "wasting the soil"
| Translator | Rendering | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "render-useless the ground" | Hyphenated verb |
| LOGOS | "render the ground useless" | Natural word order |
| PNEUMA | "wasting the soil" | Dynamic paraphrase |
| **SOPHIA** | **"render the ground useless"** | **LOGOS selected; natural English without hyphenation** |

### 13:8 — "Lord" vs. "Sir"
| Translator | Rendering | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "Lord" | Preserves theological ambiguity |
| LOGOS | "Lord" | Same |
| PNEUMA | "Sir" | Contextual address |
| **SOPHIA** | **"Lord"** | **EMET/LOGOS (2-to-1); preserves parabolic theology** |

### 13:11 — "bent together" vs. "bent-together" vs. "bent double"
| Translator | Rendering | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "bent-together" | Hyphenated |
| LOGOS | "bent together" | Unhyphenated |
| PNEUMA | "bent double" | Idiomatic English |
| **SOPHIA** | **"bent together"** | **LOGOS selected; renders *synkyptousa* without unnecessary hyphenation** |

### 13:11 — "raise herself up completely" vs. "straighten-up completely" vs. "stand up straight"
| Translator | Rendering | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "straighten-up completely" | Hyphenated |
| LOGOS | "raise herself up completely" | Full precision |
| PNEUMA | "stand up straight" | Simplified |
| **SOPHIA** | **"raise herself up completely"** | **LOGOS selected; *anakupsai eis to panteles* rendered with full force** |

### 13:12 — "you are loosed" vs. "you have been released" vs. "you are set free"
| Translator | Rendering | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "you have been released" | Perfect passive |
| LOGOS | "you are loosed" | Preserves *lyo* wordplay |
| PNEUMA | "you are set free" | Dynamic equivalent |
| **SOPHIA** | **"you are loosed"** | **LOGOS selected; maintains verbal link to vv. 15-16 (*lyei/lythenai*)** |

### 13:14 — "needful" vs. "necessary"
| Translator | Rendering | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "necessary" | Standard rendering |
| LOGOS | "needful" | Elevated register |
| PNEUMA | (omitted) | Restructured sentence |
| **SOPHIA** | **"needful"** | **LOGOS selected; consistent with SOPHIA's established usage** |

### 13:16 — "ought she not to have been loosed" vs. "was it not necessary that she be loosed"
| Translator | Rendering | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "was it not necessary [that she] be loosed" | Preserves *dei* |
| LOGOS | "ought she not to have been loosed" | Natural rhetorical question |
| PNEUMA | "should not this woman... be released" | Dynamic form |
| **SOPHIA** | **"ought she not to have been loosed"** | **LOGOS selected; natural English rhetorical question** |

### 13:19 — "a great tree" vs. "a tree"
| Translator | Rendering | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "a great tree" | Includes Sinaiticus *mega* |
| LOGOS | "a tree" | Omits *mega* |
| PNEUMA | "a tree" | Omits *mega* |
| **SOPHIA** | **"a great tree"** | **EMET selected; Sinaiticus reads ΕΙΣ ΔΕΝΔΡΟΝ ΜΕΓΑ — manuscript fidelity** |

### 13:19 — "nested" vs. "lodged"
| Translator | Rendering | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "nested" | Avian imagery |
| LOGOS | "lodged" | Formal equivalent |
| PNEUMA | "nested" | Agrees with EMET |
| **SOPHIA** | **"nested"** | **EMET/PNEUMA (2-to-1); appropriate for birds in branches** |

### 13:21 — "flour" vs. "meal"
| Translator | Rendering | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "flour" | Modern English |
| LOGOS | "meal" | Archaic rendering |
| PNEUMA | "flour" | Modern English |
| **SOPHIA** | **"flour"** | **EMET/PNEUMA (2-to-1); "flour" is the natural modern equivalent** |

### 13:24 — "for" vs. "because"
| Translator | Rendering | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "because" | Standard causal |
| LOGOS | "for" | Formal causal |
| PNEUMA | "because" | Standard causal |
| **SOPHIA** | **"for"** | **LOGOS selected; maintains formal register** |

### 13:27 — "I tell you" vs. "saying to you"
| Translator | Rendering | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "saying to you" | Participial rendering |
| LOGOS | "I tell you" | Natural direct speech |
| PNEUMA | (restructured) | Embedded differently |
| **SOPHIA** | **"I tell you"** | **LOGOS selected; natural English for direct speech** |

### 13:28 — "being cast out" vs. "being cast out outside" vs. "thrown out"
| Translator | Rendering | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "being cast out outside" | Redundant but literal |
| LOGOS | "being cast out" | Clean rendering |
| PNEUMA | "thrown out" | Colloquial |
| **SOPHIA** | **"being cast out"** | **LOGOS selected; avoids redundancy and colloquialism** |

### 13:31 — "depart" vs. "journey"
| Translator | Rendering | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "journey" | Renders *poreuou* as travel |
| LOGOS | "depart" | Formal imperative |
| PNEUMA | "Leave" | Dynamic equivalent |
| **SOPHIA** | **"depart"** | **LOGOS selected; formal imperative suits the urgency** |

### 13:33 — "I must go on my way" vs. "it is necessary for me to journey"
| Translator | Rendering | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "it is necessary [for] me... to journey" | Preserves *dei* structure |
| LOGOS | "I must go on my way" | Concise rendering |
| PNEUMA | "I must keep moving" | Colloquial |
| **SOPHIA** | **"I must go on my way"** | **LOGOS selected; concise while preserving divine necessity** |

### 13:34 — "a bird" vs. "a hen"
| Translator | Rendering | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "a bird" | Broader *ornis* |
| LOGOS | "a hen" | Specific domesticated bird |
| PNEUMA | "a hen" | Agrees with LOGOS |
| **SOPHIA** | **"a bird"** | **EMET selected; preserves the OT divine-wings allusion (Deut 32:11; Ps 91:4)** |

### 13:34 — "you were not willing" vs. "you did not wish it"
| Translator | Rendering | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "you did not wish [it]" | Softened rendering |
| LOGOS | "you were not willing" | Confrontational force |
| PNEUMA | "you were not willing" | Agrees with LOGOS |
| **SOPHIA** | **"you were not willing"** | **LOGOS/PNEUMA (2-to-1); conveys settled refusal** |

### 13:35 — inclusion/omission of "desolate"
| Translator | Rendering | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | omitted | Follows Sinaiticus |
| LOGOS | omitted | Follows Sinaiticus |
| PNEUMA | "desolate" included | Departs from Sinaiticus |
| **SOPHIA** | **omitted** | **EMET/LOGOS (2-to-1); Sinaiticus omits ΕΡΗΜΟΣ — manuscript fidelity** |

---

## Summary Statistics

- **Total disagreements logged:** 23
- **LOGOS selected as basis:** 17 times
- **EMET selected as basis:** 3 times (13:19 "great tree," 13:19 "nested" with PNEUMA, 13:34 "bird")
- **EMET/LOGOS agreement (2-to-1 vs. PNEUMA):** 5 times
- **LOGOS/PNEUMA agreement (2-to-1 vs. EMET):** 5 times
- **EMET/PNEUMA agreement (2-to-1 vs. LOGOS):** 3 times
- **Manuscript fidelity override:** 2 times (13:19 "great tree," 13:35 omission of "desolate")

---

*Compiled by SOPHIA in council with EMET, LOGOS, and PNEUMA.*
